sections.paragraph:
σωτήρ, ῆρος, ὁ [σῴζω] ‘one who liberates from real or threatening harm or loss’, savior, deliverer, benefactor applied in the larger Mediterranean world to extraordinarily beneficent figures, whether divine or human, but in NT only

—a. of the God of Israel Lk 1:47; 1 Ti 1:1; 2:3; 4:10; Tit 1:3; 2:10; 3:4; Jd 25.

—b. of Jesus Christ Lk 2:11; J 4:42; Ac 5:31; 13:23; Eph 5:23; Phil 3:20; 2 Ti 1:10; Tit 1:4; 2:13; 3:6; 2 Pt 1:1, 11; 2:20; 3:2, 18; 1 J 4:14.
